verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes HSP70-1, a canonical cytosolic/nuclear chaperone. Under baseline conditions it is predominantly intracellular (cytosol, nucleus). However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in multiple non-baseline states: (1) cancer/oncogenic transformation — tumor cells display ecto-HSP70 on their surface, a validated CAR-T and antibody target; (2) heat shock and other cellular stress — HSP70 is rapidly displayed on the PM outer leaflet; (3) immunogenic/apoptotic cell death — HSP70 is exposed on the outer face of dying cells. These documented ecto-pools make it contextual/cell_state_induced.
